privacy-policy.heading: Information we collect
privacy-policy.html:
We collect the following categories of information:

  • Player and parent information you provide during evaluations or registration — including name, date of birth, gender, contact details (email, phone, address), and emergency contact information.
  • Soccer history you choose to share, such as prior club, level of play, and goals.
  • Communications you send us via email, phone, text, or web forms.
  • Technical information automatically collected when you visit our website — including IP address, browser type, device information, pages viewed, and referring site.

privacy-policy.heading: How we use your information
privacy-policy.html:
We use the information we collect to:

  • Process evaluation requests and team registrations.
  • Match players with the appropriate coach, age band, and program.
  • Communicate with you about tryouts, schedules, and events.
  • Operate, maintain, and improve our website and services.
  • Comply with legal obligations and protect our rights.

privacy-policy.heading: Information sharing
privacy-policy.html:
We do not sell your personal information. We may share information with:

  • Service providers who help us operate the club (e.g. PlayMetrics, JotForm, email service providers) — strictly to perform their services on our behalf.
  • League and governing bodies (MLS NEXT, US Soccer, CalSouth, etc.) where required for player registration or competition eligibility.
  • Legal authorities when required by law or to protect the safety of our players, families, and staff.

privacy-policy.heading: Children's privacy (COPPA)
privacy-policy.html:
Many of our players are under the age of 13. We comply with the Children’s Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA). Personal information about players under 13 is provided by a parent or legal guardian during the evaluation or registration process. We collect only the information necessary to provide soccer programming and to communicate with the parent or guardian.

Parents and guardians may review, update, or request deletion of their child’s information at any time by contacting us using the details below.

privacy-policy.heading: Cookies and tracking
privacy-policy.html: Our website uses cookies and similar technologies to operate the site, remember your preferences, and analyze how the site is used. For details, see our Cookie Policy.

privacy-policy.heading: Data security
privacy-policy.html: We use reasonable administrative, technical, and physical safeguards to protect your information. No method of transmission over the Internet or electronic storage is 100% secure, however, and we cannot guarantee absolute security.

privacy-policy.heading: Your rights
privacy-policy.html:
Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have the right to:

  • Access the personal information we hold about you.
  • Request correction of inaccurate information.
  • Request deletion of your information.
  • Opt out of marketing communications at any time by following the unsubscribe instructions in our emails or contacting us.

To exercise any of these rights, contact us at the address below.

privacy-policy.heading: Changes to this policy
privacy-policy.html: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. The “Last updated” date at the top of this page reflects the most recent version. We encourage you to review this page periodically.

terms-of-service.heading: Acceptance of terms
terms-of-service.html: By using our website or services, you confirm that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms. If you do not agree, please do not use our site or register for our programs.

terms-of-service.heading: Eligibility
terms-of-service.html:
CVFC programs are designed for youth players. Players under the age of 18 must have a parent or legal guardian register on their behalf and accept these Terms.

CVFC reserves the right, at its sole discretion, to determine whether a player is eligible to participate in any program.

terms-of-service.heading: Registration and accounts
terms-of-service.html:
Player registration may be completed through our partner platforms (PlayMetrics for older age groups, JotForm for younger ages). You agree to provide accurate, current, and complete information and to keep that information up to date.

You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of any account credentials and for all activity that occurs under your account.

terms-of-service.heading: Player evaluations and tryouts
terms-of-service.html:
CVFC offers seasonal tryouts and year-round individual evaluations. Acceptance into any program is at the sole discretion of the CVFC coaching staff. A request for evaluation does not guarantee a roster spot.

Schedules, programs, and team assignments may change from season to season. CVFC will make reasonable efforts to communicate changes in advance.

terms-of-service.heading: Fees and payments
terms-of-service.html: Program fees are set each season and communicated during registration. Fees are non-refundable except where required by law or as expressly stated in writing by CVFC. Need-based financial assistance may be available — contact us for details.

terms-of-service.heading: Code of conduct
terms-of-service.html: All players, parents, guardians, and family members are expected to conduct themselves in a manner consistent with our core values: Passion. Unity. Respect. Attitude. Conduct that is disrespectful, abusive, threatening, or unsafe — toward players, coaches, officials, or opponents — may result in suspension or removal from the club.

terms-of-service.heading: Intellectual property
terms-of-service.html: All content on this website — including logos, text, graphics, images, and the CVFC crest — is the property of Chula Vista Fútbol Club or its licensors and is protected by copyright, trademark, and other applicable laws. You may not copy, reproduce, distribute, or create derivative works without our prior written permission.

terms-of-service.heading: Media and likeness
terms-of-service.html: By participating in CVFC programs, you grant CVFC permission to use photographs, video, and audio recordings of players taken during practices, matches, and club events for promotional and educational purposes — including the website, social media, and print materials. If you wish to opt out, please notify us in writing.

terms-of-service.heading: Limitation of liability
terms-of-service.html: Soccer involves inherent risks of injury. By registering for CVFC programs, you acknowledge those risks. To the fullest extent permitted by law, CVFC, its directors, coaches, employees, and volunteers are not liable for any injury, loss, or damage arising from participation in our programs, except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct.

terms-of-service.heading: Changes to these terms
terms-of-service.html: We may update these Terms from time to time. The “Last updated” date at the top of this page reflects the most recent version. Continued use of our website or services after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated Terms.

terms-of-service.heading: Governing law
terms-of-service.html: These Terms are governed by the laws of the State of California, without regard to its conflict of laws principles. Any disputes arising under these Terms will be resolved in the state or federal courts located in San Diego County, California.

cookie-policy.heading: What are cookies?
cookie-policy.html: Cookies are small text files placed on your device when you visit a website. They allow the site to recognize your device and remember information about your visit, such as your preferences or whether you’ve been here before. Similar technologies include local storage, pixels, and tags — referred to collectively as “cookies” in this policy.

cookie-policy.heading: Types of cookies we use
cookie-policy.html:
Strictly necessary

Required for the site to function — for example, to remember your selections during a registration flow. The site cannot work properly without these cookies, so they cannot be turned off.

Performance and analytics

Help us understand how visitors use the site (e.g. which pages are most viewed) so we can improve the experience. These cookies collect aggregated information and do not identify individual visitors.

Functional

Remember choices you make (such as your preferred language or region) to provide a more personalized experience.

Third-party

Some pages embed content or services from third parties (e.g. PlayMetrics registration, JotForm forms, embedded video). These third parties may set their own cookies. We do not control these cookies — refer to the third party’s own privacy and cookie policies.

cookie-policy.heading: Managing your cookies
cookie-policy.html:
Most web browsers allow you to control cookies through their settings. You can:

  • Accept or block all cookies.
  • Block cookies from specific sites.
  • Delete cookies that have already been set.

Please note that blocking strictly necessary cookies may prevent parts of the website from working correctly.

cookie-policy.heading: Do Not Track
cookie-policy.html: Some browsers offer a “Do Not Track” (DNT) signal. Because there is not yet a uniform standard for how websites should respond to DNT signals, our site does not currently change its behavior based on DNT.

cookie-policy.heading: Changes to this policy
cookie-policy.html: We may update this Cookie Policy from time to time to reflect changes in technology, regulation, or our practices. The “Last updated” date at the top of this page reflects the most recent version.

link-policy.heading: Links to third-party sites
link-policy.html:
Our website may contain links to third-party sites that we don’t own or control. We provide these links for convenience only. We are not responsible for the content, accuracy, privacy practices, or terms of use of any third-party site, and the inclusion of a link does not imply endorsement.

You access third-party sites at your own risk. We encourage you to review the terms and privacy policy of any site you visit through a link from ours.

link-policy.heading: Linking to our website
link-policy.html:
You may link to our publicly available pages, provided that the link does not:

  • Suggest sponsorship, endorsement, or affiliation where none exists.
  • Appear in a context that is unlawful, defamatory, or otherwise objectionable.
  • Frame or embed our content in a way that alters or obscures our branding.
  • Use our name, logo, or other marks without our prior written consent.

We reserve the right to request the removal of any link that violates these terms.

link-policy.heading: No endorsement
link-policy.html: A link to or from our website does not constitute an endorsement, partnership, or recommendation unless explicitly stated in writing.

link-policy.heading: Reporting a problem
link-policy.html: If you find a broken or inappropriate link on our site, please let us know using the contact information below. We’ll review and update as appropriate.

link-policy.heading: Changes to this policy
link-policy.html: We may update this Link Policy from time to time. The “Last updated” date at the top of this page reflects the most recent version.
